response:
[theory]  
The claim "One can crack their teeth biting a Pecan" is not correct.  
Pecans are hard nuts that are not easily broken by biting.  
Teeth are not designed to crack or break in this way.  
Biting a pecan would likely cause the nut to split but not the teeth to crack.  
The claim is based on a misunderstanding of how teeth and nuts interact.  
It is not possible to crack teeth by biting a pecan.  
The claim is not supported by common sense.  
The claim is not verifiable without a web search.  
The claim is false.  

***

The claim is false.  
The claim is not correct.
